summary: Marketing is fully focused on launching the Asia Start campaign and Intelligent China program, while the China Going-Global report with IE Business School is on a tight timeline for September events.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the Asia Start campaign, which kicked off this week with two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month, and the Intelligent China program targeting foreigners, with a website and module one in Beijing being readied for a December launch. Urgently this week, the team must expedite social media agency selection—inviting all four agencies to the next round—and fix Asia Start campaigns by switching from audience targeting to observation mode. The China Going-Global report with IE Business School, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', is on a tight timeline for early September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore; Felix needs to confirm launch opportunities and create a one-pager proposal. Additionally, the team is handling press releases for Shun Tian Shu and the Forbes China CEO list, coordinating Dean Xiang's keynote at QS Asia Pacific Summit in November, and following up on a potential collaboration with the Global Peter Drucker Forum.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Meeting to discuss SEO strategy and prioritize programs for marketing, focusing on Asia Star and Intelligent China as top priorities, with Berkeley as a potential third.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, brochure updates, CES quotes, campus video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency search, and IE report translation.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore in September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and a new Intelligent China program launching in December, focusing on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Consider Berkeley as a third priority for overseas programs.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu is needed, not two.
Media Minds is likely the best agency choice given budget constraints and time.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report to be ready by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month.
Intelligent China program targets foreigners, is in English, and focuses on module one in Beijing first.
Invite all four agencies (麦炽, 省广, Impactable, Media Minds Global) to participate in the next round of social media ad agency selection.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: Request for Proposal - social media ad agency 2026 - updated 0703

decisions.text: Use Temu as the second case study for the IE University report instead of halocoko.
Place key findings at the beginning of the IE University report.
Proceed with the shorter version (1500 words) of the article for Erica's approval.
decisions.meetingTitle: FW: 长江商学院媒体监测日报2026-7-2
